Lam Research Deepens Investment in Boise to Support Projected Growth in U.S. Semiconductor Manufacturing
data.description:
Lam Research Corp. commemorated the opening of its new office in Boise, Idaho, joined by U.S. Senator Jim Risch, representatives of Micron Technology Inc. and other distinguished local government, community, and academic leaders. The new office will initially support approximately 150 Lam personnel from the greater Boise area focused on collaborative research, development and high-volume manufacturing of Micron's memory technology, with room for future growth.

Found in fabs across the U.S. and around the globe, Lam's innovative manufacturing equipment and processes are used to create nearly every advanced chip in the world. Building on Lam's more than 30 years in Boise, the new 9,200 sq. ft.

office is the company's latest U.S. expansion and part of a multi-year strategy to increase the company's operational and innovation velocity to support chipmakers enabling the artificial intelligence (AI) era. Among Lam's customers is Boise-headquartered Micron, who recently awarded Lam as Outstanding Front End Equipment Supplier of the Year in 2025.

Blackstone Inc. (NYSE : BX) entered into a definitive agreement to acquire Champions Group Holdings from Odyssey Investment Partners, LLC.
data.description:
Blackstone Inc. (NYSE : BX) entered into a definitive agreement to acquire Champions Group Holdings from Odyssey Investment Partners, LLC on February 17, 2026. Upon completion, Odyssey and management are retaining a significant minority investment alongside Blackstone.

The transaction is expected to close in the first half of 2026, subject to customary conditions.

Weil, Gotshal & Manges LLP acted as a legal advisor to Blackstone Inc. William Blair & Company, L.L.C., Piper Sandler & Co. and Baird Financial Corporation acted as financial advisor to Champions Group Holdings and Odyssey Investment Partners, LLC . Latham & Watkins LLP acted as legal advisor to Odyssey Investment Partners, LLC.

data.title: Outdoor camera, surveillance maker Tactacam explores sale, sources say
Warner Bros Rejects Paramount's Takeover bid
data.description:
On February 17, 2026, Warner Bros Discovery announced that it has rejected Paramount Skydance Corporation's $30-per-share takeover offer but has granted until February 23, 2026, to submit a more favorable deal. In addition, Paramount suggested a higher share price of $31 per share, contingent upon Company agreeing to open negotiations. Further, Company?s Chairman Samuel DiPiazza Jr. and CEO David Zaslav reaffirmed their commitment to a merger with Netflix, stating that the Board has not determined that Paramount's proposal is likely to result in a superior transaction, and the current bid from Paramount for the entire company stands at $108.4 billion, while Netflix is offering $82.7 billion specifically for Company?s studio and streaming units.

Furthermore, the Company has indicated that it expects a best and final proposal from Paramount to exceed the current offer, and Paramount has expressed opinion over the lack of meaningful engagement from the Company regarding its previous offers and has attempted to attract the Company shareholders by proposing additional cash for each quarter the deal remains unclosed, and the Company maintains that the revised merger agreement with Paramount does not meet the criteria for a superior proposal as determined by its Board.
